what_it_does: This file is a metadata-only channel-memory attachment record that links attachment att-b3514676 to board item item-0dd81f2c in the dragnet-development channel.
observations: The file consists only of YAML frontmatter with no body content.
It records an added timestamp in ISO 8601 format.
The attachment id follows the 'att-*' naming pattern.
standouts: Empty body — the entire payload is frontmatter.
The record is purely referential with no descriptive text.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with a leading and trailing '---' delimiter.
Follows a structured metadata schema with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields.
concerns: No contextual description of what the attachment represents.
No indication of whether the target board item still exists or is current.
The meaning of 'board_items' as an attachment_type is not defined in the file.
notes: A bare channel-memory attachment stub that points to a board item but carries no explanatory content of its own.
questions: What content or action does board item item-0dd81f2c represent?
Why is the attachment body empty?
Who created this attachment and why?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose as a board_items attachment matches the content: it records a link to a board item. It does not provide any additional narrative or payload.
apparent_themes: Cross-reference and traceability between channel memory and board items.
intention_signals: It appears intended to create a durable, timestamped association between a channel memory artifact and a specific board item for later retrieval or lineage.
novelty: The use of a 'board://' URI scheme as a target reference is a distinct linking convention.
contribution: Provides a timestamped, typed link between a channel memory attachment and a board item, which a fused system could keep as a provenance edge.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter channel-memory attachment record named att-b3514676 in dragnet-development that references board item item-0dd81f2c via a board:// URI with no body content.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to persist a typed link from a channel memory attachment to a board item.
